stats_fallback.christened: 2021-11-27 (Port Rashid, Dubai, UAE — during UAE Golden Jubilee; godmother Sophia Loren, her 17th MSC ship christening)
stats_fallback.cabins_note: 2,421 total across 17 categories. Includes 95 MSC Yacht Club suites (with two-deck Royal Suite), 15 solo Interior Studios, and 60 ADA-accessible staterooms. Approximately 75% of cabins have balconies.
stats_fallback.covid_delay: Original 8 November 2020 delivery delayed by COVID-19 to 1 February 2021. Phased debut with UK-only 'Seacations' from Southampton starting May 2021 before formal November 2021 Dubai christening.
stats_fallback.yacht_club_status: Installed from 2021 delivery. 95 Yacht Club suites on Decks 16/18/19 including a two-deck Royal Suite. Private Top Sail Lounge (Deck 16), MSC Yacht Club Restaurant (Deck 18), MSC Yacht Club Grill & Bar (Deck 19), MSC Yacht Club Sundeck & Bar (Deck 19), MSC Yacht Club Pool (Deck 19), Top 19 Exclusive Solarium. 24-hour concierge and butler service.

related.class_notes: Meraviglia-Plus Class. MSC Virtuosa is the second and final ship of the sub-class. Her direct sister is MSC Grandiosa (2019). The original Meraviglia Class (MSC Meraviglia 2017 and MSC Bellissima 2019) is the parent generation at 171,598 GT; the Meraviglia-Plus pair are an enlarged derivative at 181,541 GT. MSC Euribia (2023) is a further derivative with an LNG power plant — not a direct sister. Each Meraviglia-generation ship uses different MDR names and has unique venue signatures; Virtuosa's unique features include the MSC Starship Club with Rob the humanoid robot bartender and the Indochine pan-Asian MDR (not on other Meraviglia-class ships).

deployment.winter_2025_26: 7-night Southern Caribbean round-trips from Pointe-à-Pitre, Guadeloupe (Saint Vincent, Grenada, Saint Lucia, Saint Maarten, Antigua, Saint Kitts).
deployment.apr_2026_transatlantic: Departed Pointe-à-Pitre 5 April 2026 on a 14-night eastbound transatlantic repositioning voyage bound for Europe via Ponta Delgada, Azores (expected arrival 12 April 2026). As of 11 April 2026 she is mid-Atlantic.
deployment.summer_2026: Northern Europe from Southampton, Le Havre, and Hamburg plus Mediterranean itineraries from Barcelona, Marseille, and Genoa.

history.keel_laid: 27 February 2019, Chantiers de l'Atlantique Saint-Nazaire (hull H34)
history.phased_debut: Due to COVID-19 restrictions, MSC Virtuosa did not have a traditional maiden voyage. She entered service in May 2021 with UK-only domestic 'Seacations' from Southampton while international cruising was still restricted.
history.christened: 27 November 2021, Port Rashid, Dubai, UAE (during UAE Golden Jubilee). Godmother Sophia Loren — her 17th MSC ship christening. Traditional champagne-bottle smash plus fireworks. First commercial cruise departed Dubai 28 November 2021 under Captain Francesco Veniero.
history.2025_10_27_first_drydock: 27 October 2025 — entered Damen Verolme Rotterdam for her first statutory dry dock (routine class maintenance, not a cosmetic refit).
history.2025_11_19_drydock_complete: 19 November 2025 — departed dry dock and began 17-night transatlantic repositioning to the Caribbean for the 2025-26 winter season.

discrepancies_flagged.hallucinations_removed: Previous Review JSON-LD claimed 'Cirque du Soleil at Sea', '112-metre LED sky dome', and 'two Himalayan Bridge rope courses'. The MSC × Cirque du Soleil at Sea partnership has been phased out — removed. The '112-metre LED sky dome' is the length/scale claim from older Grandiosa marketing; it's not explicitly referenced in current Virtuosa sources — replaced with the generic Galleria Virtuosa LED promenade description. Virtuosa has one Himalayan Bridge (class-signature glass-floor walkway), not two rope courses — removed.
discrepancies_flagged.guest_capacity_hallucinations: Previous page had THREE inconsistent guest figures: intro said 'approximately 6,334 guests at double occupancy' (wrong — 6,334 is MAX, not double), specs card said '4,888 guests', stats card said '6,334'. Verified: 4,810 double occupancy / 6,334 maximum.
discrepancies_flagged.beam_outlier: VesselTracker reports an anomalous 52 m beam — this is a data-entry error. Correct beam is 43 m per VesselFinder, CruiseMapper, MSC Cruises, and Chantiers de l'Atlantique. Use 43 m.
discrepancies_flagged.cirque_status_update: IMPORTANT: The prior list of 'four Cirque ships' (Meraviglia, Bellissima, Grandiosa, Virtuosa) is historically accurate but no longer operationally current as of 2026. The MSC × Cirque du Soleil at Sea partnership has been phased out. MSC's own Carousel Productions at Sea shows now run in the Carousel Lounge venues that were purpose-built for Cirque. Similar updates should flow back through any Meraviglia, Bellissima, and Grandiosa references that still mention Cirque — those pages may need review.
discrepancies_flagged.4_mdrs: Virtuosa has a unique 4-MDR combination (Blue Danube, The Opera, The Simphony, Indochine) distinct from every other Meraviglia-generation ship. The Indochine pan-Asian MDR is unique to Virtuosa. MSC's spelling of 'Simphony' with an 'i' (rather than 'Symphony' with a 'y') is MSC's official naming on the current ship map PDF.
discrepancies_flagged.cirque_rob_ship_status: MSC Virtuosa's MSC Starship Club with Rob the humanoid robot bartender remains in operation as of 2026 per MSC's own materials.
